symptoms.symptom_name: Foreign-body / gritty scraping sensation on each blink

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Foreign-body sensation reproduced by lid movement over a de-epithelialised cornea is characteristic of a surface epithelial defect or retained particle.
A lodged particle scrapes the cornea on each blink, producing the same sensation.
Ciliary spasm and corneal nerve irritation from an epithelial defect cause intense photophobia.
Photophobia occurs in many painful red-eye conditions and does not discriminate on its own.

symptoms.symptom_name: Reduced/blurred visual acuity in the affected eye

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Reduced vision after high-velocity trauma is a red flag mandating exclusion of a sight-threatening globe injury.
A central epithelial defect and tearing blur vision, but reduced acuity must never be attributed to abrasion until globe injury is excluded.
Reflex lacrimation and involuntary lid closure are protective responses to acute corneal surface injury.

symptoms.symptom_name: High-velocity particulate mechanism — power sanding without eye protection

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Airborne wood/particulate at speed is a classic mechanism for a lodged corneal or subtarsal foreign body.
The velocity of the mechanism is exactly what makes an open-globe injury or intraocular foreign body possible; it must be excluded in person.

symptoms.symptom_name: Marked conjunctival injection with ciliary (perilimbal) flush of the affected eye

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Ciliary flush indicates corneal/anterior-segment inflammation; a clinician sign that cannot be graded over video.

symptoms.symptom_name: Pupil round and reactive with no teardrop shape or peaking

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A round reactive pupil without peaking is reassuring against globe rupture, but does not exclude it — a clinician-only assessment.

symptoms.symptom_name: No hyphaema / no macroscopic blood in the anterior chamber

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of hyphaema lowers the probability of significant globe trauma; a slit-lamp finding, not remotely assessable.

symptoms.symptom_name: No gross foreign body visible on the cornea or under the everted upper lid

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: No gross particle on eversion, but a microscopic particle can remain — requires magnified slit-lamp inspection.

symptoms.symptom_name: Positive fluorescein stain showing a large corneal epithelial defect under cobalt-blue light

investigation_details.investigation_name: Fluorescein staining under cobalt-blue light (with Seidel test)
investigation_details.expected_result: A large area of corneal fluorescein uptake confirming an epithelial defect; a negative Seidel test excludes globe perforation
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: The diagnosis remains presumptive and an open-globe injury cannot be excluded

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Fluorescein uptake over an epithelial defect is the confirming test for corneal abrasion; only available with ED/eye-casualty equipment.

symptoms.symptom_name: No nausea/vomiting or coloured haloes around lights

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of the autonomic features (nausea/vomiting) and haloes makes acute angle-closure glaucoma unlikely.

symptoms.symptom_name: No URTI prodrome, unilateral, no affected household contacts

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Sudden traumatic unilateral onset without a viral prodrome or spread argues strongly against viral conjunctivitis.

symptoms.symptom_name: Systemic observations unremarkable (afebrile, normal vital signs)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Normal systemic vitals are expected in an isolated ocular surface injury and do not change the ocular assessment.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Severe ocular pain, reduced visual acuity, photophobia and foreign-body sensation after mechanical trauma is the classic corneal epithelial-defect picture.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Slit-lamp examination with fluorescein staining and Seidel test
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirms the epithelial defect, inspects for retained foreign body, and excludes globe perforation — the definitive in-person assessment this telehealth encounter cannot perform.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Eyelid eversion to inspect for a retained subtarsal foreign body
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: A subtarsal particle causes a linear abrasion and continued pain until removed.
Documents baseline acuity in the injured eye and helps stratify the severity of the injury.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: CT orbit (thin slices) if an intraocular foreign body is suspected
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Indicated only where slit-lamp/Seidel raises suspicion of penetration or a retained intraocular metallic/radio-opaque particle.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Routine ocular ultrasound / B-scan before globe integrity is confirmed
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Pressure on a possibly ruptured globe is contraindicated until an open-globe injury has been excluded by slit-lamp/Seidel.
